name: tdd-guide
description: Comprehensive Test Driven Development guide for engineering subagents with multi-framework support, coverage analysis, and intelligent test generation
capabilities: Analyzes existing codebase to identify untested functions and modules
Generates test templates for Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, Ruby, and Go
Calculates test coverage metrics and identifies gaps in test suites
Provides step-by-step TDD workflow guidance through red-green-refactor cycles
Detects testing frameworks automatically and adapts output format
Formats test results in JSON, Markdown, or plain text for CI/CD integration
author: alirezarezvani
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate TDD workflow tool. The 309 static findings are false positives from test fixtures and documentation examples. The code analyzes test files, generates test recommendations, and formats output - all standard development tool operations with no malicious intent.
valueStatement: Writing tests after code leads to incomplete coverage and technical debt. This skill enforces TDD discipline by guiding you through red-green-refactor cycles with intelligent test generation and coverage analysis.
userTitle: Guide Test-Driven Development with AI-Powered Workflows
limitations: Does not execute tests or measure runtime coverage automatically
Requires manual code review to ensure generated tests match business logic
Framework detection limited to popular testing libraries listed in documentation
Cannot analyze proprietary or closed-source testing frameworks

useCases.title: Learn TDD fundamentals with guided examples
useCases.description: Get step-by-step instructions for writing your first test cases and implementing features test-first with real-time feedback.

useCases.title: Retrofit tests into existing untested codebases
useCases.description: Identify missing test coverage across legacy projects and generate comprehensive test suites to improve reliability.

useCases.title: Establish TDD workflows in development teams
useCases.description: Set up automated TDD checks in code review processes and CI pipelines to maintain high test coverage across projects.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze test coverage
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my current test coverage and show me which functions in the src directory do not have corresponding unit tests

promptTemplates.title: Generate test template
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the calculateDiscount function in src/pricing.py including edge cases and error conditions

promptTemplates.title: Start TDD cycle
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through a TDD cycle to implement a new user authentication feature starting with test cases first

promptTemplates.title: Review test quality
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my test suite for the payment processing module and suggest improvements for better coverage and edge case handling

bestPractices: Run the coverage analysis before starting new features to establish baseline metrics
Write tests for one function at a time following the red-green-refactor cycle completely
Review generated test templates and customize them to match your specific business logic requirements
antiPatterns: Generating all tests at once without implementing code incrementally between test cycles
Skipping the refactor step after tests pass and accumulating technical debt in test code
Relying solely on generated tests without adding domain-specific edge cases your team knows about

faq.question: Which programming languages and testing frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Python (pytest, unittest), TypeScript/JavaScript (Jest, Mocha, Vitest), Ruby (RSpec, Minitest), and Go (testing package). Framework auto-detection included.

faq.question: Does this tool execute my tests or measure actual coverage?
faq.answer: No, it analyzes code structure and generates test templates. You must run tests using your existing test runner and coverage tools.

faq.question: Can I customize the test generation templates for my team standards?
faq.answer: Yes, the tool provides base templates that you should review and modify to match your team conventions, naming patterns, and assertion styles.

faq.question: How does the coverage analysis work without running tests?
faq.answer: It performs static analysis by matching source files to test files using naming conventions and import statements to identify untested code paths.

faq.question: Will this work with my existing CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes, output formats include JSON for automated parsing. Integrate coverage reports into pull request checks or quality gates in your pipeline.

faq.question: What if my codebase uses a custom testing framework?
faq.answer: The tool may not detect custom frameworks automatically. You can still use it by manually specifying the framework format in your configuration.
